strengths: Clear 7-section structure: Education, Experience, Gpa: [X.Xx] / 4.00, Interests, Objective — easy to skim and ATS-parser friendly.
Skill density of 10.7 keywords per 100 words is well above the ~5.0 average for technical resumes — strong ATS surface area.
2 project(s) listed — gives reviewers concrete artifacts beyond job titles, especially valuable for early-career applicants.
red_flags: Only 0 bullets detected — most resumes need 8-15 to establish credibility. Are sections being missed by the parser?
suggestions: Link a GitHub or portfolio so reviewers can verify project claims in 30 seconds.
Phase-1 audit flagged: Few quantified outcomes — add metrics to bullets · No GitHub/portfolio link — let reviewers verify project claims
insights.narrative:
Scanned 150 words across 0 bullets in 7 sections. The resume reads as a ~38-second skim — recruiters typically allocate 6–15 seconds, so the top third has to carry the strongest signals on its own.

On the rubric that matters most, quantification sits at 0% (industry benchmark is 60%+) and action-verb usage at 0% (target 70%+). Weak phrasings detected: 0, buzzwords: 0. Closing these gaps is the highest-leverage edit on this resume; they directly translate into interview conversion.

For IC Design / VLSI alignment: 16 hard skills extracted with density 10.7 per 100 words. First red flag worth attention: only 0 bullets detected — most resumes need 8-15 to establish credibility. are sections being missed by the parser?

Highest-leverage edits next: Link a GitHub or portfolio so reviewers can verify project claims in 30 seconds. · Phase-1 audit flagged: Few quantified outcomes — add metrics to bullets · No GitHub/portfolio link — let reviewers verify project claims
